title: Garden Route Municipalities proactively managing water resources ahead of summer season
description: GARDEN ROUTE NEWS  - The Western Cape...
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"GARDEN ROUTE NEWS  - The Western Cape Department of Local Government, in partnership with the Department of Water and Sanitation (DWS), the South African Weather Service (SAWS), and the Garden Route District and local municipalities, held a briefing session this week to evaluate water security in the district ahead of summer.","html":"GARDEN ROUTE NEWS - The Western Cape Department of Local Government, in partnership with the Department of Water and Sanitation (DWS), the South African Weather Service (SAWS), and the Garden Route District and local municipalities, held a briefing session this week to evaluate water security in the district ahead of summer."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Wouter Kriel, spokesperson for Anton Bredell, Western Cape Minister of Local Government, Environmental Affairs and Development Planning, said the meeting united all municipal managers, technical directors and key stakeholders from national and provincial spheres to examine the current status of water security within the Garden Route District."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“While rainfall for August, September and October was below average across much of the Garden Route, updated forecasts from the SAWS indicate that the outlook for the coming summer period is favourable. Above-normal rainfall is expected for the November to January period, potentially improving water availability across the region.”","html":"“While rainfall for August, September and October was below average across much of the Garden Route, updated forecasts from the SAWS indicate that the outlook for the coming summer period is favourable. Above-normal rainfall is expected for the November to January period, potentially improving water availability across the region.”"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Bredell assured residents and visitors that there is no immediate concern regarding water security in the Garden Route.","html":"Bredell assured residents and visitors that there is no immediate concern regarding water security in the Garden Route."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“We are working closely with the DWS and all local municipalities to monitor conditions and ensure that every town has access to adequate water supplies. Contingency measures and responsible water management plans are already in place to address localised infrastructure challenges,” said Bredell.","html":"“We are working closely with the DWS and all local municipalities to monitor conditions and ensure that every town has access to adequate water supplies. Contingency measures and responsible water management plans are already in place to address localised infrastructure challenges,” said Bredell."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Municipalities have implemented water demand management measures, including local water restriction levels, to promote the responsible use of water, particularly as the region prepares to welcome an influx of holidaymakers during the festive period.","html":"Municipalities have implemented water demand management measures, including local water restriction levels, to promote the responsible use of water, particularly as the region prepares to welcome an influx of holidaymakers during the festive period."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“The provincial government, together with our national and local partners, remains in full control of the situation,” added Bredell.","html":"“The provincial government, together with our national and local partners, remains in full control of the situation,” added Bredell."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“We continue to monitor water levels, infrastructure performance and rainfall patterns, and we stand ready to provide support wherever needed. Our goal is to ensure that no town in the Garden Route runs dry.”","html":"“We continue to monitor water levels, infrastructure performance and rainfall patterns, and we stand ready to provide support wherever needed. Our goal is to ensure that no town in the Garden Route runs dry.”"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"This co-ordinated effort forms part of the province’s broader commitment to building water resilience. On Friday 31 October the department will launch the Provincial Water Resilience Strategy, which aims to strengthen proactive planning and sustainable water management across all municipalities in the Western Cape.","html":"This co-ordinated effort forms part of the province’s broader commitment to building water resilience. On Friday 31 October the department will launch the Provincial Water Resilience Strategy, which aims to strengthen proactive planning and sustainable water management across all municipalities in the Western Cape."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Bredell called on residents and visitors alike to use water responsibly.","html":"Bredell called on residents and visitors alike to use water responsibly."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“Every drop counts. While conditions are expected to improve, we must all play our part to use water wisely and safeguard this precious resource.”","html":"“Every drop counts. While conditions are expected to improve, we must all play our part to use water wisely and safeguard this precious resource.”"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: Garden Route Municipalities proactively managing water resources ahead of summer season
